result.meeting_context: Regular meeting of the City of Mountain View Visual Arts Committee focused on approving prior minutes, hearing public comments, outlining upcoming agenda priorities, receiving CIP public art status updates, discussing remote meeting policy changes, refining processes for public art placement and selection, and reviewing the Center for the Performing Arts gallery call timeline.

points.speaker: Toni Hsu
points.text: Minutes for the October 12, 2022 meeting were delivered to committee members and posted on the City Hall bulletin board; after offering public comment, the committee approved the minutes via roll call vote with unanimous yes responses.
points.source: Toni Hsu : Agenda item 3 minutes Approval minutes for the October 12th, 2022 meeting have been delivered to committee members and posted on the City Hall bulletin board.

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: A member of the public (Naya) introduced herself as a local artist seeking to learn about public art; staff invited her to email for background and questions, offering direct contact for follow-up outside the meeting.
points.source: John Lang : Please e-mail me and I'll give you some background on this and I'm happy to answer your questions.

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: A public attendee reported difficulty connecting from agenda PDFs because Zoom codes were not embedded. Staff stated the issue has been raised with document processing and committed to work on resolving the inconsistency.
points.source: John Lang : I've raised it with document processing Bruce, and I'll work on getting it resolved, so thank you.

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Committee asked for guidance on responding to direct public inquiries. Staff has requested direction from the City Clerk on permissible communications and will relay clear protocols to committee members when available.
points.source: John Lang : I have a inquiry into the Clerk's office on this matter and we'll once to make clear to me what the boundaries are, I will communicate that to the VAC.

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Staff outlined upcoming topics: a holistic approach to downtown public improvements (grade separation, pedestrian mall, related CIP projects), a December update on the public art strategy, landscaping integration tied to the biodiversity plan, K5 policy augmentation, a joint meeting with the Downtown Committee, and downtown grade separation in December or February.
points.source: John Lang : …effective February meeting… The Great Separation project for downtown… possibly coming to as early as December, if not, it will be at your February meeting… we will be bringing that topic to the Committee's consideration.

points.speaker: Jesse Cupp
points.text: Committee noted City Council approved a pilot exception enabling public art in the protected bike lane on California Street, creating an opportunity to integrate art with bike infrastructure despite current K5 limitations.
points.source: Jesse Cupp : …they made an exception and they're piloting it for the bike lane… there's going to be some public art in the protected bike lane that's going down California.

points.speaker: Susi Merhar
points.text: A committee member expressed support for VAC involvement in the pedestrian mall functional plan and emphasized interest in discussing the next public art strategy iteration, including initiatives, processes, and funding mechanisms.
points.source: Susi Merhar : …very happy to hear John that gonna work on a comprehensive approach towards the pedestrian mall… I hope we can talk about the public art strategy more, especially about the initiatives and how that all ties in and what processes and and funding will work.

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Aquatic Center public art remains on track for a Fall 2023 opening; construction is a little over 25% complete. Two of three artist contracts are executed; the last is pending insurance requirements, which staff is working to finalize.
points.source: John Lang : …project is still very much on track to open Fall 20, 23. It's a little over 25% complete… two of the three contracts have been executed. We are still trying to finalize the last contract… insurance requirements…

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Fayette Park construction has commenced, with no additional updates at this time. Rankstorff Park maintenance and tennis buildings design is near completion; the construction bid is expected to go out at year end.
points.source: John Lang : …Fayette Park construction just commenced. There is no other immediate update… Rankstorff park maintenance and tennis buildings design is near completion now and we do anticipate bid… at the end of the year.

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: For Rankstorff Park murals, staff continues to work through insurance requirements on artist contracts. After agreements are finalized and Youth and Senior Advisory input is gathered, design concepts will return to the VAC for review before proceeding.
points.source: John Lang : …working through insurance requirements on contracts… Once those agreements are in place, we will be able to bring back… the design concepts after both artists had spent some time with the senior and youth advisory committees…

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: This is the last stand‑alone AB 361 item; effective February 1, 2023, advisory committees and City Council will return to in‑person meetings. December and January virtual meetings will be covered by Council action. Atrium supports hybrid public attendance; currently no vaccination requirement.
points.source: John Lang : …effective February 1, 2023, the advisory committees and City Council will be going back in person… Council will be continuing to extend these 30 day emergency orders at least through February… At the present time, no.

points.speaker: Jesse Cupp
points.text: Given Clerk direction, the committee opted not to vote on the AB 361 resolution. The December meeting will occur virtually under Council’s extension, and meetings will transition to in‑person beginning in February.
points.source: Jesse Cupp : It's just gonna like, take… time… and not accomplish anything. So we're going to 7.2.

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Staff proposed tools to improve placement decisions: use 3D modeling for larger projects; provide clearer scale comparisons; organize noticed group site visits to review contexts; and integrate park equipment details (e.g., color palettes and procurement status) into art selection to enhance cohesion.
points.source: John Lang : …could 3D modelling be used to understand the placement… use some better comparisons so people understand the scale… the committee goes out together… walk the site… bringing those elements into the conversation as it relates to art.

points.speaker: Toot Too Thomson
points.text: Committee recommended a standard checklist for artist coordination and approvals—covering color, size, materials, rotation, height, and orientation—to ensure key siting and visibility considerations are addressed while documenting when choices are left to artist discretion.
points.source: Toot Too Thomson : …build a like a standard checklist… item a color item B size, item C material item D rotation… If we say… artist has the freedom to choose, then we note that…

points.speaker: Clifford Bryant
points.text: Committee suggested leveraging prior park public engagement used for equipment selection by inviting resident input or capturing preferences during that process to inform calls for artists, avoiding duplicative outreach and improving local fit.
points.source: Clifford Bryant : …can we… incorporate those public members into selection of the public art piece that'll go into their park… invite them to see the pieces… and give comments…

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: For larger or multi‑element projects, the VAC may use an iterative process (e.g., stipends for finalists). Staff will research past per‑piece thresholds that triggered RFQ/stipend approaches and return with recommendations; artists will receive clearer insurance requirements upfront in calls.
points.source: John Lang : …happy to come back to the committee… when in the past you've kind of done this other process… not on the totality of the project, but the individual art pieces… we need to make very clear with artists there's insurance requirements.

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: To avoid surprises post‑approval, staff proposed adding contractual fabrication checkpoints (e.g., at 25%, 50%, 75%) for progress documentation and ensuring approved schematics specify dimensions and color palettes, which then attach to the contract as binding deliverables.
points.source: John Lang : …require say a quarterly like 1/4, a half a three quarter check in… and… the schematic… has that detail… it's six feet… these are the colour palettes… it gets put as an attachment.

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Group site visits can be Brown Act compliant by noticing the meeting with purpose and time (e.g., on‑site review of public art placement). Staff indicated this is an addressable process detail for future CIP art evaluations.
points.source: John Lang : …we just need to notice it as such and state exactly what is happening… we can certainly notice it as a meeting.

topics.topic: Center for the Performing Arts call

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: The 2023 Center for the Performing Arts gallery call has been open since October and closes Saturday; 29 submissions have been received. Jurying opens Sunday via CaFE. Staff asks VAC members to review before the December 13 meeting so selections can be made.
points.source: John Lang : …call for artists out since October… closes on Saturday. Currently we have 29 submissions… starting Sunday you will be receiving some emails from CAFE to start to jury… before the December 13th meeting…

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: The annual plan targets about five exhibits with two to three alternates. Due to contractual simplicity, only one artist will exhibit at a time, though combined shows may be considered in the future when staffing allows.
points.source: John Lang : So typically for the year it's at least five with two alternatives… From a contractual standpoint, it needs to be one at a time.

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Artists apply via CaFE; roughly three days remained as of the meeting. Staff will forward the call link to VAC for broader sharing. Older works are acceptable; there is no requirement that submissions be newly created.
points.source: John Lang : …three days and a couple hours left to apply… They can apply through cafe… I will forward the, I will forward this call out after this meeting to all the VAC.

points.speaker: Jesse Cupp
points.text: The meeting adjourned at 7:40 PM. Participants exchanged thanks and brief well‑wishes at closing, including noting upcoming vacation plans.
points.source: Jesse Cupp : It's 740 and this meeting is adjourned. Thank you very much.

decisions.text: Provide a public art strategy update at the December VAC meeting.
decisions.source: John Lang : …I will be bringing back more information in December on this next iteration for you…

decisions.text: Forward the Center for the Performing Arts call link to VAC members after the meeting for distribution.
decisions.source: John Lang : I will, I will forward the, I will forward this call out after this meeting to all the VAC.

decisions.text: Research past per‑piece thresholds for iterative/stipend processes and return to VAC with recommended ranges and process notes.
decisions.source: John Lang : …happy to come back to the committee… when in the past you've kind of done this other process… I'm happy to come back to the committee and socialize that…

decisions.text: Decline to take action on the AB 361 resolution, relying on Council’s extension for December and planning for in‑person meetings from February 1, 2023.
decisions.source: Jesse Cupp : It's just gonna… not accomplish anything. So we're going to 7.2.

decisions.text: Work with document processing to resolve inconsistent Zoom links in agenda PDFs.
decisions.source: John Lang : I've raised it with document processing Bruce, and I'll work on getting it resolved…

decisions.text: Obtain guidance from the City Clerk on public contact protocols and communicate the rules to VAC members.
decisions.source: John Lang : I have a inquiry into the Clerk's office on this matter… I will communicate that to the VAC.

decisions.text: Notify VAC when CPA jurying opens and request members review submissions before December 13.
decisions.source: John Lang : …starting Sunday you will be receiving some emails from CAFE to start to jury… before the December 13th meeting…
